apify.name: call-actor
apify.description: Call any Actor from the Apify Store. WORKFLOW: 1. Use fetch-actor-details to get the Actor's input schema. 2. Call this tool with the actor name and proper input based on the schema. If the actor name is not in "username/name" format, use search-actors to resolve the correct Actor first. Typically returns a datasetId and preview of output items.

actor.description: The name of the Actor to call. Format: "username/name" (e.g., "apify/rag-web-browser"). For MCP server Actors, use format "actorName:toolName" to call a specific tool.

async.description: When true, starts the run and returns immediately with runId. When false or omitted, behavior depends on the active server mode. Use async=true only when the user explicitly asks to run in the background.

callOptions.description: Optional call options for the Actor run configuration.

memory.description: Memory allocation for the Actor in MB. Must be a power of 2. Minimum: 128 MB, Maximum: 32768 MB (32 GB).

timeout.description: Maximum runtime for the Actor in seconds. After this time elapses, the Actor will be automatically terminated. Use 0 for infinite timeout.

input.description: The input JSON to pass to the Actor. Required.

previewOutput.description: When true (default): includes preview items. When false: metadata only (reduces context).

apify.name: search-actors
apify.description: Search the Apify Store to FIND and DISCOVER what scraping tools/Actors exist for specific platforms or use cases. This tool provides INFORMATION about available Actors - it does NOT retrieve actual data or run any scraping tasks. Apify Store contains thousands of pre-built Actors for all platforms and services.

keywords.description: Space-separated keywords used to search pre-built solutions (Actors) in the Apify Store. Use 1-3 simple keyword terms maximum (e.g., "Instagram posts", "Twitter", "Amazon products").

limit.description: The maximum number of Actors to return (default = 5)

offset.description: The number of elements to skip from the start (default = 0)

apify.name: fetch-actor-details
apify.description: Get detailed information about an Actor by its ID or full name (format: "username/name", e.g., "apify/rag-web-browser"). Use 'output' parameter with boolean flags to control returned information. The 'readme' field returns the summary when available, full README otherwise.

actor.description: Actor ID or full name in the format "username/name", e.g., "apify/rag-web-browser".

output.description: Specify which information to include in the response to save tokens.

mcpTools.description: List available tools (only for MCP server Actors).

metadata.description: Include developer, categories, last modified date, and deprecation status.

outputSchema.description: Include inferred output schema from recent successful runs.

stats.description: Include usage statistics (users, runs, success rate).

apify.name: get-actor-run
apify.description: Get detailed information about a specific Actor run by runId. The results will include run metadata (status, timestamps), performance stats, and resource IDs (datasetId, keyValueStoreId, requestQueueId). Use ONLY when user explicitly asks about a specific run's status or details.

runId.description: The ID of the Actor run.

apify.name: apify--rag-web-browser
apify.description: Web search and fetch tool for AI agents and RAG pipelines. It queries Google Search, scrapes the top N pages using a full web browser, and returns their content as clean Markdown for further processing by an LLM. Can also fetch individual URLs.

maxResults.description: The maximum number of top organic Google Search results whose web pages will be extracted. If `query` is a URL, then this field is ignored.

outputFormats.description: Select one or more formats to which the target web pages will be extracted and saved in the resulting dataset.

query.description: REQUIRED Enter Google Search keywords or a URL of a specific web page. The keywords might include the advanced search operators.

apify.name: get-actor-output
apify.description: Retrieve the output dataset items of a specific Actor run using its datasetId. You can select specific fields to return (supports dot notation like "crawl.statusCode") and paginate results with offset and limit.

datasetId.description: Actor output dataset ID to retrieve from.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include (supports dot notation like "crawl.statusCode"). For example: "crawl.statusCode,text,metadata"

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 100).

apify.name: fetch-apify-docs
apify.description: Fetch the full content of an Apify or Crawlee documentation page by its URL. Use this after finding a relevant page with the search-apify-docs tool.

url.description: URL of the Apify documentation page to fetch. This should be the full URL, including the protocol (e.g., https://docs.apify.com/).

apify.name: search-apify-docs
apify.description: Search Apify and Crawlee documentation using full-text search. You must explicitly select which documentation source to search using the docSource parameter.

docSource.description: Documentation source to search. Defaults to "apify".

limit.description: Maximum number of search results to return. Defaults to 5. Maximum is 20.

offset.description: Offset for the search results. Defaults to 0.

query.description: Algolia full-text search query to find relevant documentation pages. Use only keywords, do not use full sentences or questions.
